HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN UNDER EITHER PRONG OF AGUILAR. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. DEFENDANT FAILED TO NEGATE AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. EVEN IF ADMISSIBLE, MR. MADEIROS' DECLARATION, WHEN READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY, MERELY CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WORKED AT A SITE AT WHICH DEFENDANT ALSO WORKED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: RHONDA R. HARRIS, CSR #7414

Off Calendar Aug-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Paul E. Hausknecht of HAKE LAW PC (415-364-0370), appearing for defendant / moving party Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Raymond Barragan

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Even if admissible, Mr. Madeiros' declaration, when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony, merely creates a triable issue whether plaintiff worked at a site at which defendant also worked. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Rhonda R. Harris, CSR #7414 (503/TLJ)
